news.title: Hayallerimiz, öfkelerimiz ve Kadir İnanır
news.summary: Kadir İnanır 58 yıllık kariyerine 182 film, 12 dizi sığdırdı. Yeşilçam'ın en üretken ama Türkiye'nin en sancılı dönemlerinde kamera karşısındaydı.

news.title: Türk sinemasının usta ismi Kadir İnanır hayatını kaybetti
news.summary: Türk sinemasının usta ismi Kadir İnanır, tedavi gördüğü hastanede hayatını kaybetti. 77 yaşındaki yıldız oyuncu 14 Mayıs'ta zatürre rahatsızlığı nedeniyle hastaneye kaldırılmış, kısa bir süre sonra da akciğerindeki enfeksiyon nedeniyle yoğu

news.title: Avrupa kavruluyor: Almanya, Belçika ve Hollanda'da sıcaklık rekorları

news.title: Venezuela'da peş peşe iki deprem: Can kaybı 920'ye yükseldi
news.summary: Venezuela'da 24 Haziran'da peş peşe meydana gelen 7,2 ve 7,5 büyüklüğündeki iki depremde en az 589 kişi öldü, 3000'e yakın yaralı var. Ülke genelinde olağanüstü hal ilan edildi.

news.title: Rutte'nin İran saldırılarına üs desteği sözleri Tahran'ı kızdırdı, Roma iddiaları yalanladı
news.summary: NATO Genel Sekreteri Mark Rutte, İran'a yönelik "Epik Öfke" operasyonunu desteklemek için İtalya'daki ABD üslerinden 500 ABD uçağının, Avrupa çapında da binlercesinin havalandığını söyleyince NATO ile İran arasında gerginliğe neden oldu.

news.title: YKS'de hatalı soru tartışması, ÖSYM inceleme başlattı. Edebiyat ve matematikte iptaller olacak mı?
news.summary: Yükseköğretim Kurumları Sınavı'nda (YKS) bu yıl tartışma yaratan iki soru için Ölçme, Seçme ve Yerleştirme Merkezi (ÖSYM) inceleme başlattı.

news.title: Ankara'da terör operasyonu. 178 kişi tutuklandı
news.summary: Ankara merkezli yürütülen terör operasyonlarında gözaltına alınan 225 şüpheliden 178'i, sevk edildikleri mahkemece tutuklanarak cezaevine gönderildi.

news.title: Trafikte görülmemiş hız cezası. Formula 1 ve yolcu uçağından bile süratli çıktı
news.summary: Saattle bin 221 kilometre hızla radara yakalanan ve hız cezası yiyen 62 yaşındaki adama 30 bin liralık ceza tebligatı geldi. Mahkemeye başvuran yaşlı adam yanlış kesilen cezayı iptal ettirdi. Haber: Rojbin Hayrullahoğlu

news.title: Makas kazasında iki otomobil dereye uçtu
news.summary: Bursa'da trafikte makas atan aracın neden olduğu zincirleme kazada iki otomobil dereye uçtu, 6 kişi yaralandı.

news.title: Haziran ayı enflasyonu ne zaman açıklanacak?

news.title: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
news.summary: US Central Command says it has struck missile and drone storage facilities and coastal radar positions.

news.title: Venezuela earthquakes kill 920 people as international rescue teams arrive
news.summary: Hundreds are still feared trapped under the rubble, as families desperate for news.

news.title: Mother dies saving daughter in Venezuela earthquakes
news.summary: Héctor Bello wrote on social media that "you gave your own life for our daughter" in the quakes which killed at least 920 people.

news.title: Meloni and Trump: A very public fall-out that is proving very hard to fix
news.summary: Italy's PM was not long ago being called the "Trump whisperer", but their relationship has gone from public attacks to personal insults.

news.title: Europe's deadly heatwave breaks German record and halts public events
news.summary: Germany's highest ever temperature of 41.3C is recorded provisionally in Saarbrücken, over the border from France.

news.title: Religion row as Texas makes Bible stories required reading in schools
news.summary: Critics say the new reading requirements infringe on religious freedoms and blur the separation of church and state.

news.title: Which teams have qualified for the World Cup 2026 knockouts, round of 32?
news.summary: The 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout stage format, criteria and rules for qualification. Find out who's in and who's out.

news.title: Which teams are in the World Cup round of 32, and what’s the schedule?
news.summary: Cape Verde's stunning debut headlines the team list for the World Cup knockouts, which begin on Sunday in Los Angeles.

news.title: Iran wait on World Cup progress after late goal disallowed in Egypt draw
news.summary: Egypt finish second with five points in Group G, while Iran finish third with three points.

news.title: Fishermen capture the moment earthquake hits Venezuelan coast
news.summary: Fishermen have captured the moment twin earthquakes hit Venezuela’s La Guaira coastline on Wednesday.

news.title: Cape Verde becomes tiniest nation to make World Cup knockouts
news.summary: Cape Verde erupted in celebration after the national team qualified for the World Cup knockout stage and made history.

news.title: US strikes on Iran test fragile ceasefire
news.summary: The US has struck Iran for the first time since the two sides reached a Memorandum of Understanding on June 17.

news.title: U.S. strikes Iran in response to a drone attack on a ship
news.summary: The U.S. struck Iran on Friday in response to a drone attack a day earlier on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. It's the most significant test yet to an interim understanding reached a week ago by the two countries.

news.title: Pete Buttigieg and his kids subject to CPS, police investigation after false report
news.summary: Buttigieg, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential contender, shared his family's experience when police and Child Protective Services came to his home investigating an anonymous tip that was false.

news.title: 5 million have dropped ACA insurance after Trump and the GOP let prices skyrocket
news.summary: The number of people who signed up for a health plan and then didn't pay their premium fell much more sharply than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.

news.title: A day after Alito's testy response to Sotomayor's dissent, court says it was a 'misunderstanding'
news.summary: During Supreme Court opinions Thursday, Justice Samuel Alito, who wrote the majority opinion in an asylum case, appeared to rebut Justice Sonia Sotomayor, who wrote the dissent. The move was highly unusual — and on Friday there was a coda.

news.title: It's not just Cape Verde: African soccer is having a World Cup moment
news.summary: The soccer greats — Brazil, England, Spain — have been giving uneven performances out on the pitch during this World Cup. Meanwhile, teams from Africa have been relentless.

news.title: She posted about ICE. Five months later, DHS agents told her to take her post down
news.summary: In January, Paigelynne Gonyea posted about the immigration surge in Minneapolis. This week, she was visited by ICE officials who claimed one of her posts had doxxed a federal agent.

facts.text: Honey never spoils — edible honey has been found in ancient tombs.
A day on Venus is longer than its year.
Bananas are berries; strawberries are not.
